manifest.author: Shirayuki Nekomata
manifest.description: This is a plugin for Obsidian (https://obsidian.md) for Amazing Marvin (https://app.amazingmarvin.com/)
manifest.name: Amazing Marvin

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- You can now choose the **default view** for the ribbon leaf: **List**, **Calendar (Week)**, or **Calendar (Month)**.  
- The ribbon leaf now includes a **Calendar** tab with Week and Month modes alongside the existing List tab.  
- Settings UI adds a **Ribbon leaf** section with a **Default view** dropdown and clearer account/ribbon labels.  
- The ribbon leaf opens with the selected default view and respects the ribbon show/hide setting.

## Behind the scenes
- Added a typed setting (`LeafDefaultView`) and validation when loading settings to ensure a safe default.  
- Improved settings redraw handling so leaf redraws catch and warn instead of throwing.  
- Refactored ribbon query settings and toggle labels for clearer behavior and maintainability.
releases.name: Release 1.0.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- You can now switch between the legacy task list and a Calendar tab within the leaf so you can browse scheduled tasks for a week or a month before pushing them into your notes.
- The new calendar display refreshes gracefully, highlights today, and keeps the ribbon-based list available so you can toggle between live lists and the fuller calendar mode.
- Daily-note actions from the calendar leaf now behave just like the ribbon command, letting you drop selected tasks into the note you’re working on.

## Behind the scenes
- The plugin metadata and release docs were bumped to version 1.0.0 to match the new calendar experience.
- Calendar UI styles and the rich-note parser’s error handling were polished so the new view feels stable and recovers cleanly from malformed note data.
releases.name: Release 1.0.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- Fixed the Update button fallback and hangs: the plugin no longer becomes stuck when clicking "Update". Network requests now abort after a timeout and overlapping requests are canceled, so the UI recovers and subsequent actions succeed.
- Avoid app crashes on empty subtasks: items with null/undefined `subtasks` are now normalized safely (no more Object.values(null) exceptions).
- Live Preview behavior improved: code blocks render only when appropriate — in reading mode, or in Live Preview only if the cursor isn't inside the block (editing won’t cause render/refresh conflicts).

## Behind the scenes
- Improved fetch reliability and request concurrency handling to avoid UI hangs from slow or stuck HTTP requests.
- Cleaned up lifecycle and resource handling to prevent leaks and improve view/unload stability.
- Minimum supported Obsidian version has been raised to 1.10.3; users running older Obsidian may need to upgrade to use the latest plugin build.
releases.name: Release 0.3.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix `labels` API can return a 500 error code
releases.name: Release 0.3.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adding more features for the plugin:

### Add a command
- You can now add scheduled for today or due tasks into your daily notes

### Add a ribbon
- Clicking on ribbon will open a leaf to show tasks scheduled today / due items
- Customizable settings
- Add ability to create daily note and add tasks in list

### Quality of life improvements:
- Add a last time updated to refresh button
- Add animated icons
- Better transitioning animations

### Bug fixes
- Closes #13
releases.name: Release 0.3.0
